docs.title: Classmate App Documentation
docs.text: Welcome to the official documentation for the Classmate App — a cross‑platform Flutter application designed for classroom communication, collaboration, and future real‑time chat features.

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text: The Classmate App follows a modular Flutter architecture.

docs.title: Layers
docs.text:
• UI Layer (Widgets)
  • Logic Layer (Controllers/Providers)
  • Data Layer (Services, Firebase)

docs.title: Navigation Flow
docs.text: • main.dart → HomePage → Feature Modules

docs.title: Future Architecture
docs.text:
• Firebase Authentication
  • Firestore/Realtime Database
  • WebRTC for calls

docs.title: Developer Guide
docs.text: This guide helps developers contribute to the Classmate App.

docs.title: Adding a New Screen
docs.text:
1. Create a new Dart file in lib/
  2. Add a widget class
  3. Register route in main.dart

docs.title: State Management
docs.text: Planned options: - Provider - Riverpod - Bloc

docs.title: Adding Features
docs.text:
• Create a folder under lib/features/
  • Add UI, logic, and models

docs.title: What is the Classmate App?
docs.text: The Classmate App is a cross-platform Flutter application designed for classroom communication, collaboration, and real-time chat. It connects students and teachers for seamless classroom interactions.

docs.title: What platforms does it support?
docs.text: The app is built with Flutter and supports: - Android (via APK or Google Play) - iOS (via App Store) - Web (optional, via Flutter Web) - Windows, macOS, Linux (desktop versions planned)

docs.title: Is the app open-source?
docs.text: Yes! The project is open-source and available on GitHub. We welcome contributions from the community.

docs.title: How do I run the app?
docs.text:
Use: 

flutter run

Make sure you have an emulator running or a physical device connected.

docs.title: How do I set up the development environment?
docs.text: Follow the Setup Guide for detailed instructions on installing Flutter, cloning the repo, and running the app.

docs.title: What version of Flutter do I need?
docs.text:
Flutter 3.x or later is required. Check your version: 

flutter --version

docs.title: Can I use VS Code instead of Android Studio?
docs.text: Yes! VS Code works great with Flutter. Just install the Flutter extension.

docs.title: How do I add a new screen?
docs.text: Create a new Dart file in lib/screens/ or lib/features/, add a widget class, and register the route in main.dart. See Developer Guide for details.

docs.title: What state management library should I use?
docs.text:
The project supports multiple options: - Provider — lightweight and simple - Riverpod — improved version of Provider - Bloc — powerful for complex apps

Choose based on your use case. See Architecture.

docs.title: How do I contribute to the project?
docs.text: Follow these steps: 1. Fork the repository 2. Create a feature branch (git checkout -b feature/my-feature) 3. Commit your changes (git commit -am 'Add my feature') 4. Push to the branch (git push origin feature/my-feature) 5. Submit a Pull Request

docs.title: How do I report a bug?
docs.text: Open an issue on GitHub with: - A clear title - Steps to reproduce - Expected vs. actual behavior - Screenshots (if UI-related)

docs.title: How do I set up Firebase?
docs.text:
Run: 

flutter pub add firebase_core firebase_auth cloud_firestore
flutter pub get

Then configure Firebase credentials in lib/firebase_options.dart.

docs.title: Is Firebase required?
docs.text: No, Firebase is optional. The app includes scaffolding for it, but you can use other backends. See Architecture.

docs.title: How do I test without Firebase?
docs.text:
Yse Firebase Emulator Suite: 

firebase emulators:start

docs.title: "flutter: command not found"
docs.text: Add Flutter to your PATH environment variable. See Setup Guide.

docs.title: Build fails with "Gradle sync failed"
docs.text:
Run: 

flutter clean
flutter pub get

docs.title: App crashes on startup
docs.text: Check: 1. flutter doctor for missing dependencies 2. flutter pub get to install packages 3. Logcat output: adb logcat | grep flutter

docs.title: iOS build fails
docs.text:
Try: 

cd ios
pod repo update
pod install
cd ..
flutter run

docs.title: How do I improve app performance?
docs.text:
• Use const constructors for widgets
  • Profile with DevTools: flutter pub global activate devtools && devtools
  • Lazy-load images and data
  • Use ListView.builder() for large lists

docs.title: Can I build for production?
docs.text:
Yes! Use: 

# Android
flutter build apk --release

# iOS
flutter build ios --release

docs.title: What features are planned?
docs.text: See the Roadmap for a detailed timeline.

docs.title: When will feature X be released?
docs.text: Check the Changelog and Roadmap for planned release dates.

docs.title: Can I request a new feature?
docs.text: Yes! Open a GitHub issue with "Feature Request" as the title and describe your idea.

docs.title: More Questions?
docs.text: If your question isn't answered here: - Check the User Guide - Review the Developer Guide - Search existing GitHub issues - Open a new GitHub discussion

docs.title: Project Roadmap
docs.text: This roadmap outlines the planned development phases for the Classmate App. It provides a clear view of upcoming features, improvements, and long‑term goals for the project. The roadmap is divided into short‑term, mid‑term, and long‑term milestones.

docs.title: 🔹 Core UI & Navigation
docs.text:
• Improve the base Flutter UI
  • Add consistent theming and typography
  • Implement scalable navigation (GoRouter or Navigator 2.0)

docs.title: 🔹 Documentation Expansion
docs.text:
• Add Setup, Features, Architecture, Developer Guide
  • Add screenshots and diagrams
  • Improve mkdocs navigation

docs.title: 🔹 Developer Experience
docs.text:
• Clean folder structure
  • Add linting rules
  • Add basic unit tests

docs.title: 🔹 Firebase Integration
docs.text:
• Firebase Authentication (Email/Password, Google Sign‑In)
  • Firebase Realtime Database or Firestore setup

docs.title: 🔹 Real‑Time Chat Module
docs.text:
• One‑to‑one chat
  • Group/class chat
  • Typing indicators
  • Message read receipts

docs.title: 🔹 Classroom Management Tools
docs.text:
• Create/join class
  • Student list
  • Announcements
  • Basic assignments module

docs.title: 🔹 State Management Upgrade
docs.text:
• Introduce Provider, Riverpod, or Bloc
  • Modularize features under lib/features/

docs.title: 🔹 Notifications
docs.text:
• Push notifications using Firebase Cloud Messaging
  • In‑app alerts for messages, assignments, announcements

docs.title: 🔹 Advanced Assignment System
docs.text:
• File uploads
  • Teacher grading tools
  • Submission tracking

docs.title: 🔹 Dashboard & Analytics
docs.text:
• Teacher dashboard
  • Student progress analytics
  • Class activity insights

docs.title: 🔹 Multi‑Platform Optimization
docs.text:
• Desktop UI improvements
  • Web performance tuning

docs.title: 🔹 AI‑Powered Features
docs.text:
• Smart attendance
  • Automated assignment feedback
  • Chat summarization
  • AI‑powered study assistant

docs.title: 🔹 Multi‑Tenant Support
docs.text: • Multiple schools/institutions under one backend

docs.title: 🔹 Offline Mode
docs.text:
• Local caching for chat and assignments
  • Sync when online

docs.title: 🏷️ Versioning Plan
docs.text: Version Features v1.0 Core UI, Navigation, Documentation v1.5 Authentication, User Profiles v2.0 Chat Module, Classroom Tools v3.0 Notifications, Assignments v4.0 AI Features, Multi‑Tenant Support

docs.title: 🤝 Contributing to the Roadmap
docs.text: Developers and contributors are welcome to propose new features or improvements. Submit ideas through GitHub Issues or Pull Requests.

docs.title: Project Setup Guide
docs.text: This guide explains how to set up the Classmate App development environment on your machine. Follow these steps if you want to contribute, modify, or run the project locally.

docs.title: 1. Prerequisites
docs.text: Before starting, make sure you have the following installed:

docs.title: Required Software
docs.text:
• Flutter SDK (3.x or later)
  • Git
  • Android Studio or VS Code
  • Java JDK 11+ (for Android builds)
  • Xcode (for iOS development on macOS)
  • Android Emulator or physical Android device

docs.title: Check Flutter Installation
docs.text:
flutter doctor

This command will verify that Flutter, Dart, and other dependencies are correctly installed. Fix any issues reported by flutter doctor before proceeding.

docs.title: 2. Clone the Repository
docs.text:
Clone the Classmate App repository to your local machine:

git clone https://github.com/yourusername/classmate-app.git
cd classmate-app

docs.title: Dart/Flutter Packages
docs.text:
Install all Dart packages defined in pubspec.yaml:

flutter pub get

docs.title: System Dependencies (Firebase)
docs.text:
If using Firebase, configure Firebase CLI:

npm install -g firebase-tools
firebase login

Then initialize Firebase for your project:

flutter pub add firebase_core firebase_auth cloud_firestore
flutter pub get

docs.title: On Android Emulator
docs.text:
flutter emulators --launch Pixel_5  # or your emulator name
flutter run

docs.title: On iOS Simulator (macOS)
docs.text:
open -a Simulator
flutter run

docs.title: On Physical Device
docs.text:
Enable USB Debugging on your device and run:

flutter devices  # List connected devices
flutter run -d <device_id>

docs.title: Run with Specific Flavor
docs.text:
If your project has build flavors (dev, staging, prod):

flutter run --flavor dev -t lib/main_dev.dart

docs.title: 5. Project Structure
docs.text:
lib/
├── main.dart              # App entry point
├── core/                  # Core utilities, constants, themes
├── models/                # Data models
├── screens/               # UI screens
├── services/              # Firebase, API services
├── widgets/               # Reusable widget components
└── features/              # Feature modules (chat, auth, etc.)

docs.title: Run Tests
docs.text: flutter test

docs.title: Code Analysis
docs.text: flutter analyze

docs.title: Format Code
docs.text: flutter format lib/

docs.title: Build Release APK (Android)
docs.text: flutter build apk --release

docs.title: Build iOS App
docs.text: flutter build ios --release

docs.title: "flutter: command not found"
docs.text: Add Flutter to your PATH. See Flutter Installation Guide.

docs.title: Build failures
docs.text:
1. Run flutter clean
  2. Run flutter pub get
  3. Run flutter pub upgrade
  4. Check flutter doctor for missing dependencies

docs.title: Android build issues
docs.text:
• Update Android SDK: flutter upgrade
  • Sync Gradle: ./gradlew clean (in android/ folder)
  • Check Java version: java -version (should be 11+)

docs.title: iOS build issues
docs.text:
• Update pods: cd ios && pod repo update && pod install && cd ..
  • Clear Xcode build: rm -rf ~/Library/Developer/Xcode/DerivedData/*

docs.title: 8. Next Steps
docs.text:
• Read the Developer Guide for contributing
  • Check Architecture for project structure
  • Review Features for current and planned features
  • See Roadmap for development timeline

docs.title: State Management
docs.text: The Classmate App will use Provider or Riverpod.

docs.title: Why?
docs.text:
• Scalable
  • Testable
  • Easy to maintain
